figures.title: ebola: Extracted Transmission Models Overview
figures.caption: Overview of 513 transmission models extracted for ebola. (A) Distribution of model architectures. (B) Classification by stochastic or deterministic formulation. (C) Primary transmission routes incorporated. (D) Proportion of models with publicly available source code.

figures.title: Interventions and Model Assumptions
figures.caption: Interventions and assumptions in extracted ebola transmission models (n=513). (A) Frequency of intervention types evaluated across models. (B) Common modelling assumptions. Categories are not mutually exclusive; individual models may incorporate multiple interventions or assumptions.

figures.title: Programming Languages
figures.caption: Programming languages used for model implementation among 246 models with specified language.

figures.title: Model Type by Formulation
figures.caption: Distribution of deterministic and stochastic formulations across model types (n=513).
panels: Model architectures stratified by stochastic/deterministic formulation

figures.title: ebola: Compartmental Model Structures
figures.caption: Distribution of compartmental model structures among extracted ebola transmission models (n=513). The most common structure was Other compartmental (35.9%).
